steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The astronomer can see 'most but not all' stars with a 160 mm aperture. That means exactly one star must require more than 160 mm, and the rest must require 160 mm or less.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a different aperture size': Too vague — doesn't identify which specific star exceeds 160 mm or confirm the others don't. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an unrelated star property': Discusses something other than aperture requirements, so it can't support the 'most but not all' aperture claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'only HSH95-46 requires >160 mm': Identifies exactly one star (HSH95-46) as requiring an aperture greater than 160 mm, confirming the rest do not. That satisfies 'most but not all.' ✓
  • Choice D — 'distance rather than aperture': Refers to a different measurement entirely, not aperture requirements. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
